rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers are declared or visible in the same annex scope before use.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning helper declarations with the types of their expressions or using valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool).
Avoid placing invalid guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for output bindings instead.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are declared or visible in the same annex block.
Use only valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in const and eq declarations to prevent type resolution errors.
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes using assign for output features or auxiliary variables.
Validate that expressions in guarantees have consistent types to avoid '<error>' type mismatches.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes are for bindings.
Assignments in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not reference undefined identifiers in guarantee expressions or equations.
Do not mix invalid or mismatched types in AGREE declarations or expressions.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for output bindings.
